comments: 8 cygnets and 2 adults at the port of Leith.
2 at st Mark's park and Warriston cemetery, 1 by Roseburn.
The most common passerine heard and seen along the long route
Hanging about on the north east side.

Est
Roosting very far away, may include birds like Sanderling.

comments: And quite a large group of ducklings. Pair without young very close to causeway.

Pair briefly but headed off soon after first light. Drake - diving duck, mainly white with black mask and stripes on breast. Duck - greyish, too distant to get much detail. Very late for this species, but confident of ID.
Late bird. Light grey back clearly demarcated from black nape.

comments: Two adults, at least four cygnets
Maybe 3 but definitely 2, less fluty more metronomic and longer song than the Blackcaps.
Large flock of males resting on sandbar in mouth of River Almond, and some family groups with young
At least 200 loafing around on sandbar towards Rosyth.

Very silvery coloured crow. Had slightly more silvery colouring across the body than a previously seen hybrid that also was very silver. Although differentiating between the two can be difficult. At Gypsy Brae. Photographed.
Being aggressively chased off by Herring Gulls through Grassmarket
